name: StarTrack
description: Community star ratings for movies and TV shows — stored privately on your Jellyfin server.
overview: StarTrack adds a compact star rating pill to every Movie and TV Series detail page in your Jellyfin library. Click it to expand a full panel where you can submit your 1-5 star rating and see a breakdown of every individual user's score. All data lives in a JSON file on your server — nothing leaves your network.

versions.changelog: Diagnostic tooling for Letterboxd sync. New 🔍 Diagnose button in the My Ratings overlay runs the same library query the matcher uses and shows you the total movie count, whether the fallback query was needed, and the first 20 movies with both their original and normalized titles — so you can see at a glance whether StarTrack can see your library and how your titles are being normalized. New GET /Plugins/StarTrack/Letterboxd/Diagnose endpoint returns this data as JSON for scripting. Also adds INFO-level request logs at the top of the Import and SyncNow endpoints so you can verify in the Jellyfin server log that the request is actually reaching the plugin.

Letterboxd matcher rewritten. Previous versions ran a separate library query per CSV row with InternalItemsQuery.Years as a filter, which silently returned 0 results on some Jellyfin setups and was also slow. New approach: fetch every Movie from the library ONCE per import, build an in-memory dictionary keyed by normalized title, then match everything against that dictionary. Much faster and handles year drift cleanly (exact year → ±1 year fallback). Also adds a library fallback query that re-fetches without type filter and filters in-memory by GetBaseItemKind — covers the case where IncludeItemTypes returns nothing. The import result now includes libraryMovieCount, and the status line in both the overlay and the small widget shows it next to the import summary so you can immediately see whether the library query is working at all. Every unmatched title is logged at Debug level with its normalized form so you can diagnose matching gaps from the Jellyfin server logs.

Three Letterboxd UX fixes reported against 1.1.1. (1) Letterboxd settings are now also available from the big 'My Ratings' full-page overlay — new gold '⚙ Letterboxd' button next to Close opens an inline settings panel with the username field, auto-sync toggle, Save / Sync now buttons, and file upload, so you don't have to dig into the small rating pill to configure sync. (2) CSV import now accepts the Letterboxd export ZIP directly — just drag in the .zip you downloaded from letterboxd.com, the server extracts ratings.csv (with diary.csv as a fallback) automatically. No more manually extracting the archive. (3) Title matching is far more forgiving: strips diacritics so 'Amélie' matches 'Amelie', normalizes leading/trailing 'The/A/An' so 'The Matrix' matches 'Matrix, The', and treats apostrophes / colons / Unicode dashes / curly quotes / ampersands as word separators. Sync and import also surface a list of unmatched titles under the status line so you can see exactly which films from Letterboxd weren't found in your library. No schema/endpoint changes, safe in-place upgrade from 1.1.1.

Letterboxd sync UX fixes: (1) replaced the floating gear icon with an inline '⚙ Letterboxd sync' button at the bottom of both the item-rating view and the recent-ratings view, so it no longer overlaps the 'View all →' button and is always clearly discoverable; (2) fixed the back button getting stuck on the Letterboxd view — now always restores the correct primary view based on whether a movie is currently selected; (3) fixed the rating pill not reappearing when navigating from admin dashboard back to home — the hash cache is now invalidated when we hide on admin/video pages so the next nav check re-runs cleanly; (4) fixed the 'stale black bar' that persisted when the panel was closed with the Letterboxd view open — both the click-outside handler and the next pill-open now reset the view state explicitly. No schema/endpoint changes, safe in-place upgrade from 1.1.0.

Letterboxd sync — each user can now connect their Letterboxd account and pull their ratings into StarTrack. Click the gear icon in the rating panel on any movie to open Letterboxd settings: paste your Letterboxd username, toggle hourly auto-sync (pulls new ratings from your public RSS feed), or upload your Letterboxd CSV export for a full-history one-shot import. Films are matched to your Jellyfin library by title + year; unmatched films are reported back. A new 'StarTrack Letterboxd Sync' scheduled task runs hourly in Dashboard → Scheduled Tasks. Also: hide the floating rating pill on admin dashboard, server settings, metadata editor, scheduled tasks, plugins catalog, library settings, user edit and per-user preferences pages — the pill now only shows on Movie/Series/Episode detail pages. The sidebar nav entry still appears everywhere. Adds MIT LICENSE and third-party attribution notes.

Fix: stars badge on My Ratings cards now uses a solid near-black background with bright gold text and a gold border — readable over any poster image. Card title and meta text also have stronger drop-shadows for legibility.

Redesign: red/black theme throughout My Ratings — dark topbar with red accent border, boxed pill tab buttons (All/Movies/TV Shows/Episodes) with red active state, red play-circle on card hover, red stars badge. Repo cleanup: removed Tampermonkey script, old File Transformation code, and stale ZIPs.

Fix: sidebar 'My Ratings' link now injects by finding the navMenuSection parent container directly — works with all Jellyfin sidebar customisations including KefinTweaks. Redesign: My Ratings cards are now Letterboxd-style poster-only with gradient overlay, stars badge, and a play-arrow reveal on hover. Topbar separated from scroll area for cleaner layout.

Fix: sidebar 'My Ratings' link now injects reliably using broader nav selectors and inserts before the User section. Fix: rating pill no longer appears during video playback. New: My Ratings is now a solid-background full page (no see-through). New: tab buttons (All / Movies / TV Shows / Episodes) to filter your library by type. The sort dropdown applies within each tab.

New: personal ratings library overlay — click 'My Ratings' in the sidebar to open a full-screen Letterboxd-style grid of everything you've rated with 10 sort options (newest/oldest rated, film year ↑↓, your rating ↑↓, avg rating ↑↓, length ↑↓). New: 'My Ratings' sidebar link injected into the Jellyfin nav. Fix: recent ratings pill on home screen now shows only your own ratings instead of all users.

New: text reviews — add an optional written review when submitting a star rating. Click a username in the ratings list to expand their review. New: recent ratings panel — when browsing the home screen or library (no item selected), the widget shows a live feed of the latest ratings across all users with item names and review previews. Fix: page badge now injects on its own row above the IMDb/MDBList badges instead of colliding inline.

Fix: compile against Jellyfin.Controller 10.11.6 (exact match for server version) instead of 10.11.7 which caused NotSupported/plugin load failure due to ABI mismatch. All v1.0.10 fixes included: IAuthorizationContext for user ID, no IUserManager.GetUserById, net9.0 target.

Breaking fix: upgraded from Jellyfin 10.9/net8 to Jellyfin 10.11/net9 to match current Jellyfin releases. Removed IUserManager.GetUserById call which caused MissingMethodException on Jellyfin 10.10+ (method signature changed). Username now resolved from JWT claims instead. Ratings saving should now work on Jellyfin 10.11.x.

Fix ratings saving: switched to IAuthorizationContext (Jellyfin-native API) to resolve user ID — eliminates 401 errors on POST/DELETE. Fix half-star visual with clip-path instead of overflow:hidden. Add Episode support (widget now shows on individual episode detail pages). Improve save error logging in browser console.

Fix ratings saving: corrected user ID claim lookup to use ClaimTypes.NameIdentifier so POST/DELETE endpoints correctly identify the user. Added half-star ratings (click left/right half of each star for .5 increments). Added explicit Submit button — stars select a pending rating, button sends it. Added flash feedback on save (green success/red error). Added average rating pill next to IMDb badge on detail page.

Complete injection rewrite: replaced File Transformation reflection approach with ASP.NET Core IStartupFilter + response middleware. The middleware intercepts every index.html HTTP response and injects the widget script tag directly — no file system permissions needed, no third-party plugins required. File Transformation plugin is no longer needed.

Fix: File Transformation registration now happens synchronously inside StartAsync instead of on a background thread. File Transformation finalises its callback list early during startup — registering on a background thread meant we always missed the window. This is why JavaScript Injector worked (it registers synchronously) but StarTrack did not.

Diagnostics: callback invocation counter, status, payload type and contents length now shown in /Plugins/StarTrack/Debug. Console.log added to widget.js so browser devtools shows whether the script loads and what item IDs it detects.

Fix: TransformationPatches.SetContents now wraps the modified HTML in a JValue before assigning it back to the JObject payload, fixing the silent ArgumentException that prevented File Transformation from injecting the script tag. Also: debug report now shows the real assembly version instead of hardcoded string.

Fix: File Transformation now receives a proper JObject payload (built via reflection from the already-loaded Newtonsoft.Json assembly) instead of a POCO that caused a cast exception. This is the root cause of the widget not appearing when index.html is read-only.

Diagnostic: added /Plugins/StarTrack/Debug endpoint to report injection status without needing server logs. Injection now tries multiple known Linux web paths (/usr/share/jellyfin/web, /usr/lib/jellyfin/web, etc.) in addition to the configured WebPath. Retries injection after 5 s delay. All injection log messages upgraded to Warning level so they are always visible. File Transformation registration now logs available method names if RegisterTransformation is not found.

Fix: removed Newtonsoft.Json dependency that was preventing the plugin from loading. Added File Transformation plugin integration for non-destructive index.html injection. Fixed web path resolution using IApplicationPaths.WebPath.
Initial release — star ratings for Movies and TV Shows, community average display, expandable per-user score list, and remove-rating support.
